chapter_title.en: Chapter 54 – Description of the marking (the sacrificial animal) and the yoking (Taqleed)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/197)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/197)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: إِذَا كَانَتِ الْبُدْنُ كَثِيرَةً قَامَ فِيمَا بَيْنَ ثِنْتَيْنِ ثُمَّ أَشْعَرَ الْيُمْنَى ثُمَّ الْيُسْرَى وَ لَا يُشْعِرُ أَبَداً حَتَّى يَتَهَيَّأَ لِلْإِحْرَامِ لِأَنَّهُ إِذَا أَشْعَرَ وَ قَلَّدَ وَ جَلَّلَ وَجَبَ عَلَيْهِ الْإِحْرَامُ وَ هِيَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ التَّلْبِيَةِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Jameel Bin Darraj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘When there are many camels, stand in what is between the two, then mark the right side, then the left, but do not mark it ever until you are ready for the Ihraan, because when one marks, and yokes, and decorates, the Ihraam would be Obligatory upon him, and it is at the status of the Talbiyya’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Jamil ibn Darraj who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When there are many al-Budn, one stands in between the two, then marks on the right and then on the left. Never mark before you are ready to assume the state of Ihram because when it is decorated, something placed around its neck or marked, assuming the state of Ihram becomes obligatory upon you and it is like Talbiyah.’”
